title: besseya cinerea
intro: Besseya cinerea, also known as the cinereous beaked-corydalis, is a rare, flowering plant species native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Besseya cinerea are said to include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, among other conditions. Besseya cinerea is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, as well as tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
